name: Arizona Private Lender Association - APLA
description_short: Trade association for Arizona private money lenders offering real estate-secured loans. Connects borrowers with member lenders through a centralized loan request platform.
description_long: The Arizona Private Lender Association (APLA) was founded to serve the needs of Arizona-based private money lenders who provide loans secured by real estate using private capital sources. Rather than directly originating loans, APLA functions as a professional trade association and networking organization for private lending businesses operating in Arizona. The organization is structured as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit corporation in compliance with Arizona law. APLA maintains a membership roster of licensed private lenders and affiliated professionals who work within the private lending space. The association facilitates connections between borrowers and multiple lenders simultaneously through its centralized loan request submission system. Borrowers can complete a single form on APLA's website and have their loan request distributed to all member lenders at once, streamlining the process of obtaining quotes from multiple private sources. APLA distinguishes itself by providing standardized conduct guidelines, industry education, and professional development opportunities for its member lenders. The association also serves a coordination function with state and federal agencies on behalf of its members' collective interests. APLA's membership includes established private lending firms such as Colonial Capital LLC, Hilton Financial Corp., American Life Financial, Capital Fund I LLC, and Lukrom, among others. Members typically range from individual principals to small lending entities specializing in real estate-secured private loans. The organization's value proposition centers on professional standards, peer networking, and simplified access to capital for borrowers seeking non-traditional real estate financing. APLA is best suited for commercial real estate developers, property investors, and borrowers unable to secure traditional bank financing who need expedited capital backed by real property collateral.
pros: Centralized loan request system distributes applications to all member lenders simultaneously, saving time for borrowers
Members are reputable, licensed private lenders with established track records in Arizona real estate lending
501(c)(3) nonprofit structure and formal bylaws provide organizational legitimacy and governance standards
Association coordinates with state and federal agencies to stay current on regulatory requirements
Membership includes established firms like Colonial Capital LLC and Hilton Financial Corp. with public contact information
Hosts networking and educational opportunities to keep members current with industry trends and best practices
Accepts both individual borrower loan requests and allows members to contact lenders directly
cons: APLA itself does not originate or underwrite loans—it is purely a referral platform requiring borrower to work with individual member lenders
No pricing, interest rate, or terms information provided on website; rates and conditions vary entirely by individual lender
Limited transparency on member lender track records, complaint history, or performance metrics beyond basic contact details
Membership list appears to have duplicates (Colonial Capital LLC and Hilton Financial Corp. listed multiple times), suggesting website maintenance issues
No published standards for member conduct, disclosure requirements, or dispute resolution process available on public-facing site
best_for: Commercial real estate investors and developers seeking fast, non-traditional financing secured by property collateral
Borrowers unable to qualify for traditional bank mortgages who need multiple private lender quotes simultaneously
Property flippers and fix-and-flip investors requiring quick capital deployment for time-sensitive real estate transactions
diagnosis: APLA is a trade association and referral network, not a direct lender. It is best for commercial real estate investors and borrowers who need to quickly contact multiple private lenders simultaneously through a single loan application. The main caveat is that APLA provides no direct loan products, pricing information, or member performance guarantees—borrowers must evaluate and negotiate individually with each member lender.
services: Centralized loan request submission form distributed to all member lenders
Membership directory with contact information for licensed private money lenders
Regular membership tier for primary business owners and employees in private lending origination
Affiliate membership tier for individuals who regularly work with private money lenders
Guest access for prospective members interested in learning about membership eligibility
Industry networking and peer exchange opportunities for staying current with lending trends
Coordination and advocacy with state and federal agencies on behalf of member lenders
Professional development and educational programming on private lending best practices
Access to association bylaws and governance documents for 501(c)(3) nonprofit operations
meta_description: APLA connects borrowers with Arizona private money lenders for real estate-secured loans.
